economic_notes: Deer Cable provides cable, phone and internet services. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership revenue from provincial gaming revenue sharing (1.70% of aggregate provincial gross gaming revenue). Economic Development segment active. First Nation is economically dependent on ISC funding under Treaties.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Province is Ontario. 13 consolidated departments/segments including Deer Cable (telecom enterprise) and Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership. Deferred revenue is very large at $20.9M representing 66% of total liabilities. Audit report dated January 12, 2023, late filing noted - First Nation did not comply with First Nations Financial Transparency Act posting deadline of July 29, 2022. Landfill/contaminated site liability note appears truncated. expenses_housing combines Band Housing ($1,749,721) and CMHC Housing ($419,121). Capital segment expenses were only $138 (vs $9.9M budget). Total government transfers calculated as: ISC $26,627,837 + OFNLP $782,795 + MCSS $2,400,871 + NAN $520,362 + MHLTC $297,521 + SLAAMBP $228,437 + CMHC $386,415 + Additional govt $1,834,057 = $33,078,295 less recovery $0; OSR = rent $625,424 + interest $2,463 + other revenue $3,126,538 - net deferred ($5,321,794) = complex due to deferred revenue mechanism; OSR approximated as rent + interest + other revenue = $3,754,425 minus net deferred adjustment.
warnings: High ISC dependency (84.5%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
